Varma, who has been in exceptional touch across multiple formats and competitions, showed no signs of fatigue or distraction as he dismantled the opposition attack.

His India A tri-series exploits had already elevated his stock considerably, and this TG20 masterclass arrives at a time when India's T20I selectors are weighing their options ahead of a busy international calendar. The innings will almost certainly intensify calls for Varma to be given an extended run in the national side.

At just 22 years old, Varma is increasingly being spoken of as India's next big white-ball batting force. An innings of 136 off 56 balls, in whatever competition, sends an unmistakable message — one that the selectors will find very hard to look past.
key_facts: Tilak Varma scored 136 off 56 balls in TG20
Innings came days after India A's tri-series win
Varma is 22 years old and among India's hottest T20 prospects

stories.headline: France vs Iraq FIFA World Cup match suspended at Philadelphia; restart timeline confirmed
stories.what: The FIFA World Cup group-stage match between France and Iraq at Philadelphia was suspended mid-game, halting one of the tournament's marquee contests. The stoppage, which drew immediate attention globally, was caused by factors reported by Hindustan Times, with officials confirming when the match would resume. The suspension is a rare occurrence at a FIFA World Cup and raised significant questions about logistics and on-field management.
stories.lens: FIFA World Cup matches are watched by millions in India — a suspended game involving a heavyweight like France directly affects how the tournament's group standings and scheduling unfold.
stories.article:
A FIFA World Cup match between France and Iraq at Philadelphia was suspended during play, marking one of the most unusual stoppages in the tournament's recent history. The halt brought the high-profile group-stage contest to a temporary standstill, with officials scrambling to confirm conditions for a restart.

The reasons behind the suspension, as reported by Hindustan Times, were significant enough to prevent play from continuing in the normal course. Philadelphia, one of the host venues for the expanded 2026 FIFA World Cup being held across the United States, Canada and Mexico, became the centre of attention as news of the stoppage spread rapidly.

Officials confirmed a timeline for when the match would restart, providing some clarity to fans and broadcasters who had tuned in to watch one of the stronger European sides in action. France, perennial contenders at the World Cup, were among the most-watched teams in the tournament.

The suspension is likely to prompt a closer look at match-day protocols and stadium conditions at World Cup venues. For the global audience, and particularly the vast Indian viewership that tracks the FIFA World Cup closely, the episode underscored how even the world's biggest sporting event is not immune to unexpected disruptions.
key_facts: France vs Iraq FIFA World Cup match suspended at Philadelphia
Match is part of the 2026 FIFA World Cup
Restart timeline was confirmed by officials

stories.headline: Cocktail 2 Crosses Rs 76.25 Crore Globally in Just Three Days
stories.what: Shahid Kapoor's Cocktail 2 has delivered a strong opening weekend at the box office, grossing Rs 76.25 crore globally by the end of its third day. The film has established itself as one of the bigger Bollywood openers of 2026, riding strong domestic and overseas numbers. The three-day collection signals sustained audience interest beyond the opening-day rush.
stories.lens: Cocktail 2's Rs 76-crore three-day haul is a meaningful signal that mid-budget Bollywood films can still pull audiences to theatres — something the industry has been anxiously watching after a rough patch.
stories.article:
Cocktail 2, starring Shahid Kapoor, has stormed to Rs 76.25 crore in global box office collections through Day 3, cementing its status as one of Bollywood's stronger openers this year. The film has sustained audience momentum well past its opening Friday, a crucial indicator of genuine word-of-mouth traction rather than a front-loaded debut.

Domestic multiplexes have reported healthy occupancy through the weekend, with the film drawing particularly well in metro cities. The overseas circuit has contributed a meaningful share to the global gross, reflecting Shahid Kapoor's consistent pull with diaspora audiences.

The original Cocktail (2012), also starring Kapoor alongside Deepika Padukone and Diana Penty, was a sleeper hit of its era. The sequel has clearly benefited from that nostalgia while finding its own audience, with early social media response skewing positive for both Kapoor's performance and the film's visual treatment.

With weekday numbers now in focus, trade observers will watch whether Cocktail 2 can hold above the Rs 100-crore domestic mark — a threshold that has proved elusive for several recent Bollywood releases. A clean run without major new competition in the coming week could make that target achievable.
key_facts: Rs 76.25 crore global gross in 3 days
Starring Shahid Kapoor
Sequel to the 2012 film Cocktail
Strong metro multiplex occupancy reported over the weekend

stories.headline: Dia Mirza Says Getting a 'Dream Debut' While Deepika Padukone Worked 28 Hours Non-Stop Is Her 'Sore Spot'
stories.what: Actress Dia Mirza has spoken candidly about the contrasting early career experiences of her generation of actresses, noting that while Deepika Padukone received what she describes as a 'dream debut', Mirza herself worked gruelling 28-hour shifts with little recognition. Mirza called it her 'sore spot', acknowledging the unequal opportunities that shaped careers in Bollywood during that era. The remarks add to a growing body of honest reflection from veteran actresses about the industry's treatment of women.
stories.lens: Dia Mirza's candid admission about unequal opportunities in Bollywood puts a personal face on a structural conversation the Indian film industry has been slowly, reluctantly having about how women's careers are made or broken.
stories.article:
Dia Mirza has opened up about what she calls her 'sore spot' — the stark difference between her own grinding early career and what she describes as Deepika Padukone's 'dream debut' in Bollywood. Speaking to The Indian Express, Mirza revealed that she worked 28 hours non-stop during her early days, a sacrifice that she feels went largely unacknowledged compared to the smoother trajectory enjoyed by some of her contemporaries.

Mirza, who debuted in the early 2000s after winning the Miss Asia Pacific title, was part of a cohort of actresses who entered Bollywood at a time when the industry's machinery was far less transparent about how opportunities were allocated. Her comments implicitly highlight how debut films, production backing, and marketing investment varied dramatically depending on factors not always in an actress's control.

Deepika Padukone made her Bollywood debut in Om Shanti Om (2007) opposite Shah Rukh Khan — a launch pad that few debutantes in any era could match. Mirza's framing of this disparity as a 'sore spot' rather than bitterness is notable; she appears to be contextualising her own path rather than attacking Padukone's success.

The remarks arrive at a moment when conversations about gender equity in Bollywood — from pay gaps to how debut opportunities are structured — have become more mainstream. Mirza's willingness to name the imbalance personally adds weight to what has often remained an abstract industry discussion.
key_facts: Dia Mirza says she worked 28 hours non-stop in her early career
Describes Deepika Padukone's entry as a 'dream debut'
Calls the disparity her 'sore spot'
Deepika Padukone debuted in Om Shanti Om (2007) opposite Shah Rukh Khan

stories.headline: South OTT This Week: Baahubali: The Torchbearer, Arjun Sarja's Blast and Lingam Among Key Releases
stories.what: Several notable South Indian OTT titles are streaming this week, headlined by Baahubali: The Torchbearer — a new chapter in the iconic franchise — alongside Arjun Sarja's action film Blast and the thriller Lingam. The week also includes the family drama Breakfast, giving viewers across languages a varied menu of regional content. These releases reflect the continued strength of South Indian original content as a key driver of OTT viewership in India.
stories.lens: Anything bearing the Baahubali name commands national attention, and its OTT arrival this week will test whether the franchise's digital spin-offs can sustain the cultural momentum of the original films.
stories.article:
South Indian OTT content dominates streaming queues again this week, with Baahubali: The Torchbearer leading a lineup that also features Arjun Sarja's action entertainer Blast, the thriller Lingam, and the family drama Breakfast among the nine new releases catalogued by Pinkvilla.

Baahubali: The Torchbearer is the most high-profile arrival, carrying the weight of one of Indian cinema's most successful franchises. The S.S. Rajamouli-created universe has a proven fanbase across language barriers, and any new content within that world — whether a series or a feature — tends to generate cross-regional interest far beyond its Telugu-language origins.

Arjun Sarja's Blast, meanwhile, caters to the action-thriller audience that has consistently embraced South Indian cinema on OTT, a segment that has driven subscriber growth for multiple platforms. Lingam adds another thriller to a genre that South Indian filmmakers have been executing with particular sharpness in recent years.

The breadth of this week's South Indian releases — spanning action, family drama, and franchise content — underscores how regional OTT content has moved from being a supplementary category to a primary draw, shaping subscriber decisions across the country.
key_facts: Baahubali: The Torchbearer streaming this week
Arjun Sarja's Blast also releasing on OTT
Lingam and Breakfast among other South Indian titles this week
Nine South OTT releases total catalogued for the week

stories.headline: OTT This Week: 'Raja Shivaji', 'House of the Dragon' Season 3 and 'Gram Chikitsalay' Season 2 Among New Releases
stories.what: The week of June 22–28, 2026 brings a packed slate of OTT releases across platforms, headlined by the historical drama Raja Shivaji, the eagerly awaited House of the Dragon Season 3, and the return of the rural medical drama Gram Chikitsalay in its second season. The lineup spans Indian regional content, international prestige television, and multiple South Indian titles, giving streaming subscribers a broad range of choices this week.
stories.lens: With House of the Dragon Season 3 and multiple Indian originals dropping in the same week, streaming platforms are competing hard for viewer time — directly influencing which services Indians feel are worth keeping paid subscriptions for.
stories.article:
The week of June 22 to 28, 2026 shapes up as one of the busier OTT weeks of the month, with platforms releasing a diverse mix of Indian and international content simultaneously. Leading the charge is House of the Dragon Season 3, the HBO fantasy series that continues the Game of Thrones universe and remains one of the most globally anticipated shows on streaming.

On the Indian originals front, Raja Shivaji brings a historical drama lens to the legacy of the Maratha king, catering to an audience that has shown strong appetite for period storytelling — a genre that has thrived on OTT since Tandav and The Empire proved its viability. Gram Chikitsalay returns for a second season, its rural healthcare setting having carved a niche following in its debut run.

South Indian OTT content also figures prominently this week, with titles including Baahubali: The Torchbearer and Arjun Sarja's Blast and Lingam among the new additions, underlining how South Indian content continues to dominate streaming charts and drive subscriber numbers for regional-language platforms.

For viewers navigating multiple subscriptions, the week offers genuine reasons to explore across platforms. With so many titles competing for attention between June 22 and 28, the shows that generate the strongest word-of-mouth in the first 48 hours will likely define which ones break into broader cultural conversation.
key_facts: House of the Dragon Season 3 drops this week
Raja Shivaji (historical drama) is a new Indian OTT release
Gram Chikitsalay Season 2 returns
South Indian titles include Baahubali: The Torchbearer, Blast, and Lingam
Releases span June 22–28, 2026

stories.headline: 'Ten-Year-Old Shah Rukh Khan Would Supply Samosas to Us at NSD': Pankaj Kapur
stories.what: Veteran actor Pankaj Kapur has shared a warm memory of a young Shah Rukh Khan at the National School of Drama (NSD) in New Delhi, recalling that a ten-year-old Khan would bring samosas for students at the prestigious theatre institute. The anecdote offers a rare glimpse into Shah Rukh Khan's earliest brush with the world of performance arts, long before he became Bollywood's biggest star. Kapur, himself a distinguished NSD alumnus, made the revelation in a conversation reported by The Indian Express.
stories.lens: This anecdote humanises one of India's most mythologised celebrity success stories — reminding audiences that Shah Rukh Khan's connection to the arts began in the most ordinary, grounded way imaginable.
stories.article:
Shah Rukh Khan's journey to becoming Bollywood's biggest star may have started earlier — and more modestly — than most fans realise. Veteran actor Pankaj Kapur has recalled that a ten-year-old Khan used to supply samosas to students at the National School of Drama (NSD) in New Delhi, where Kapur was a student at the time.

The NSD connection is significant. Khan later enrolled at the institution himself, training under some of India's finest theatre practitioners before making his television debut and eventually storming Bollywood. Kapur's anecdote suggests the young Khan was orbiting that world of serious performance long before he formally entered it — soaking in an environment that would prove foundational.

Pankaj Kapur, one of India's most celebrated character actors and a product of the NSD tradition, has known Khan across multiple phases of their careers. His recollection, shared in conversation with The Indian Express, has the texture of genuine institutional memory rather than polished PR storytelling.

The story has resonated widely because it cuts against the mythology of overnight stardom — showing instead that Shah Rukh Khan's relationship with Indian theatre and performance was woven into his childhood in Delhi, years before Fauji or Deewana made him a household name.
key_facts: Pankaj Kapur recalls ten-year-old SRK supplying samosas at NSD
National School of Drama, New Delhi is the setting
Kapur describes it as his 'sore spot' of nostalgia
Shah Rukh Khan later formally trained at NSD before entering Bollywood
